page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-800s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this server utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via an 800 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the processor demonstrates distinct performance profiles depending on the workload scale. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance metric reached 3201.49, placing it in the top tier (top 10%) of tested servers. Conversely, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance scored 50410.87, which aligns with the average (middle 50%) tier. This variance indicates strong single-threaded execution capabilities, while multi-threaded throughput remains moderate under shared CPU allocation.

The qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is characterized by its high memory density and integrated NVMe storage relative to its shared CPU resource model. Because the CPU allocation is shared, the server offers a cost-effective option for memory-intensive applications that do not require continuous, dedicated multi-core processing power. This resource balance makes the instance suitable for memory-optimized workloads, database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose applications requiring large memory footprints alongside fast local storage.
description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-800s-amd is an x86_64 server from the Optimized Cloud Compute family, configured with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on a shared AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and is bundled with an 800 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. In benchmark evaluations, the instance achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3201.49, placing it in the top tier (top 10%), while its multi-core performance score of 50410.87 falls within the average (middle 50%) range. This combination of high memory capacity, fast local storage, and strong single-core performance offers a cost-effective profile for memory-heavy operations. The server is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-optimized workloads that can operate efficiently within a shared CPU allocation model.
